taskAttempted: Build a portfolio-visible static site called ThinkPad Build Observatory over 24 supervised local-model turns.
rawArtifactQuality: Partial but useful. The raw run produced documentation, data sketches, HTML/CSS, and several planning notes, but the final raw app was not publication-ready.
codexRepairBurden: High. Codex repaired invalid JavaScript, incorrect/fake seed data, a fenced validator script, and the final presentation layer inside a separate codex-fix boundary.
validationStatus: Repaired artifact passed syntax, JSON, redaction, and browser smoke checks in the final Codex phase. Raw artifact failed JavaScript and validator syntax checks.

tokenCostEstimate.estimate: No provider billing. Time cost was dominated by 24 local CPU-bound turns capped at five minutes each.

lessonsLearned: Direct-file Markdown and micro-prompts were the most reliable prompt shapes.
Broad JSON envelopes and larger context dumps increased malformed output and timeout risk.
Small local models can produce useful provenance and partial static assets, but final publication still needs Codex verification.
Raw output should remain immutable once the worker phase ends so failures stay observable.
recommendedFutureUseCases: Small static UI slices that Codex can later verify and repair

taskAttempted: Build Local Agent Lab Scoreboard, a static dashboard comparing local and agent workflows, seeded from Task 1 evidence.
rawArtifactQuality: Broken runtime with useful documentation. All turns returned responses, but raw JSON and JavaScript were fenced or malformed, so Codex repaired a separate copy.
codexRepairBurden: High. Codex created valid data, rewrote the runtime app, added a real validator, and kept raw evidence unchanged under state/.
validationStatus: Raw artifact failed JSON and JavaScript syntax checks. Repaired artifact is the portfolio-visible version.

tokenCostEstimate.estimate: Local Ollama run with no API billing. Wall-clock worker time was about 59.9 minutes across 24 turns.

lessonsLearned: The model completed every turn after the model was pulled locally, but response-format compliance stayed weak.
Switching to micro-direct prompts reduced context size but did not stop Markdown fencing in code files.
A scoreboard should track format failures separately from timeouts because completed turns can still produce broken artifacts.
Codex repair should be expected for any public-facing artifact from this small worker.
